summary: ALL credentials serialized into a single env var (OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT) and passed to workspace subprocesses
details: packages/opencode/src/control-plane/workspace.ts:570 serializes ALL auth state via JSON.stringify(yield* auth.all()) into an OPENCODE_AUTH_CONTENT env var for workspace child processes. This means every single credential (API keys, OAuth tokens, session tokens) is dumped into a single process.env variable that leaks to every spawned subprocess, appears in /proc/PID/environ, and is inherited by all child processes. This is the single largest secret leak vector in the codebase — a trivial 'ps aux' or '/proc/*/environ' read exposes everything. Fix: remove env passthrough, use IPC or FD-based credential passing instead.

summary: OAuth access and refresh tokens stored in SQLite without encryption at rest
details: AccountTable stores access_token and refresh_token as plaintext text columns in SQLite. Anyone with filesystem access to ~/.local/share/opencode/data/ can extract all OAuth tokens. This is in contrast to the desktop GitHub token which uses Electron safeStorage for encryption at rest. Fix: encrypt token columns using platform keychain or a derived encryption key.

summary: Console cloud functions log raw OAuth tokens and API keys to stdout
details: Multiple files in packages/console/ log raw secrets to stdout/console.log, including: OAuth responses with access tokens (auth.ts:106), Stripe webhook payloads (routes/stripe/webhook.ts:20), raw HTTP bodies (log-processor.ts:78), and API keys (core/script/create-api-key.ts:111). In Cloudflare Workers, stdout goes to production logs visible to all team members with log access. Fix: sanitize all console.log calls in production code, or remove them entirely.
